task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Metrics Dashboard** (Level 8)

ARCHETYPE: dashboard. Shape: web + backend, has a PAGE, and that page MUST contain 3 CHART nodes (2 is the threshold for 'dashboard' archetype; this task deliberately wants 3 to stress it). The distinguishing signal from kpi: multiple charts, not headline numbers. DO NOT build a KPI page with just `display sum_of_amount` — you must use actual `chart 'Title' as pie showing data` / `chart 'Title' as line showing data` / `chart 'Title' as b
final_source:
build for web and javascript backend

database is local memory

create a Orders table:
  amount (number), required
  status, default 'pending'
  created_at_date, auto

owner is 'test@example.com'
allow signup and login

# Backend endpoints
when user calls POST /api/orders sending data:
  requires login
  validate data:
    amount must not be empty
  saved = save data to Orders
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/orders:
  orders = get all Orders
  send back orders

# Frontend dashboard page
page 'Dashboard' at '/':
  requires login
  
  heading 'Metrics Dashboard'
  
  on page load get orders from '/api/orders'
  
  # Chart 1: Pie chart of status breakdown
  pie chart 'Order Status Breakdown' showing orders by status
  
  # Chart 2: Line chart of orders over time
  line chart 'Orders Over Time' showing orders by created_at_date
  
  # Chart 3: Bar chart of amount by status
  bar chart 'Total Amount by Status' showing orders by status

  section 'Add New Order':
    amount_input = 0
    status_select is 'pending'
    
    'Amount' is a number input saved as amount_input
    'Status' is a dropdown with ['pending', 'processing', 'completed', 'cancelled'] saved as status_select
    
    button 'Add Order':
      get orders from '/api/orders'
      send { amount: amount_input, status: status_select } to '/api/orders' as POST

test 'viewing orders endpoint':
  can user view all Orders
  expect it succeeds

test 'dashboard page loads':
  can user view the dashboard
  expect it succeeds
